mappings.rationaleAI: Traces the historical rise of manufacturing (B) and fabrication industries (C), explaining how textiles, steel, and chemicals became the backbone of industrial economies.
Engineering principles underpin aerospace (B.1), automotive (B.2), and electronics (C.6) industries, connecting design theory to industrial production.
The chemical industry (D.1) and processing sectors—plastics (D.5), rubber (D.6), dyes and pigments (D.8)—rely on the reactions and principles this volume introduces.
Organic chemistry drives the petroleum (D.2), plastics (D.5), rubber (D.6), and man-made fibre (D.9) processing industries.
Materials science connects the steel industry (C.2), textile industry (C.1), and plastics (D.5), explaining how molecular structure determines industrial performance.
A core text on D.4 (the pharmaceuticals industry), tracing the pipeline from drug discovery and development through manufacturing and regulation.
Industrial research and development (I) drives competitive advantage across manufacturing, and this volume examines how innovation processes unfold in practice.
Economic forces—trade, labor costs, capital flows—shaped the evolution of manufacturing (B) and processing industries (D) across historical periods.
Manufacturing (B) and service industries (F) are organized within capitalist market structures, and this volume explains how capital, labor, and competition interact.
The clothing and footwear industry (B.3) and textile industry (C.1) span the journey from design through production, where aesthetics meets industrial process.
The lumber industry (C.8) and paper industry (D.3) depend on forest resources, linking natural resource management to industrial output.
Biotechnology and synthetic biology are transforming the pharmaceuticals industry (D.4) and cosmetics and personal care industry (D.11).
Merchandising and marketing of consumer goods (H) connects industrial production to the consumers who purchase the output.
Environmental regulation shapes the chemical (D.1), petroleum (D.2), and surface-coating (D.7) processing industries, imposing costs and driving cleaner technologies.
Security and protection systems (F.5) represent a growing segment of the service industries, driven by technological advances in monitoring.
Utilities industries (G), including electric power generation and distribution, supply the energy on which all major industries depend.
